NavDescription:
Meg is a doctoral student in the Counseling Psychology program at UAlbany and has worked as a Prevention Navigator on Project ACCESS since the Fall of 2022. Meg is passionate about promoting harm reduction on UAlbany’s campus and providing students with a safe space to discuss their well-being. In her free time, Meg enjoys reading, rock climbing, photography, and spending time with friends and family.
Pronouns: She/her/hers
VirtualInfo: Virtual Appointments are on FRIDAYS ONLY (Video on is required)

NavDescription: Alex M. Ray is a Ph.D. student in the counseling psychology program at UAlbany and recently joined Project ACCESS as a Prevention Navigator. Alex's research interests broadly focus on substance misuse, psychological assessment, and psychometrics. In his free time, he enjoys reading and collecting spider-man comic books, watching movies, and playing guitar. Pronouns: he/him/his
Choose a navigator to view their bio and schedule an appointment!
VirtualInfo: If a Navigator's schedule is booked, try another Navigator! Look below the Navigator's Bio for their availability for virtual appointments

NavDescription: Amanda Fowler is a recent graduate of Binghamton University’s Master of Social Work Program. She earned her bachelor’s degree in human development & psychology with a minor in Sociology from University at Albany - SUNY. Amanda is passionate about advocating for and implementing harm reduction resources and strategies on college campuses. She is currently Project Manager for Project ACCESS. In her free time, she loves to play video games, watch football, workout, and spend time with loved ones. Pronouns: she/her/hers
VirtualInfo: Virtual Appointments are on WEDNESDAYS ONLY (Video on is required)
